RulePack.Name: CERT Secure Coding Rulepack, C language (combined)
RulePack.Description: CERT Secure Coding Rulepack, C language (combined)

SemanticRule.VulnCategory: MSC30-C. Do not use the rand function

Description.Abstract: MSC30-C. Do not use the rand function
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule MSC30-C. More information can be found in MSC30-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in MSC30-C.

SemanticRule.VulnCategory: INT37-C. Arguments to character handling functions must be representable as an unsigned char

Description.Abstract: INT37-C. Arguments to character handling functions must be representable as an unsigned char
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule INT37-C. More information can be found in INT37-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT37-C.

SemanticRule.VulnCategory: EXP04-A. Do not perform byte-by-byte comparisons between structures

Description.Abstract: EXP04-A. Do not perform byte-by-byte comparisons between structures
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Recommendation EXP04-A. More information can be found in EXP04-A.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in EXP04-A.

SemanticRule.VulnCategory: INT06-A. Use strtol() to convert a string token to an integer

Description.Abstract: INT06-A. Use strtol() to convert a string token to an integer
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Recommendation INT06-A. More information can be found in INT06-A.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT06-A.

ControlflowRule.Notes:
UNFINISHED - unknown how to check to make sure if ==NULL clause of fgets() return results in undefined buf.
Maybe structural would be better than control
ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: FIO40-C. Reset strings on fgets() failure

Description.Abstract: FIO40-C. Reset strings on fgets() failure
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule FIO40-C. More information can be found in FIO40-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in FIO40-C.

ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: FIO39-C. Do not read in from a stream directly following output to that stream

Description.Abstract: FIO39-C. Do not read in from a stream directly following output to that stream
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule FIO39-C. More information can be found in FIO39-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in FIO39-C.

ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: FIO33-C. Detect and handle input output errors resulting in undefined behavior

Description.Abstract: FIO33-C. Detect and handle input output errors resulting in undefined behavior
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule FIO33-C. More information can be found in FIO33-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in FIO33-C.

ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: FIO44-C. Only use values for fsetpos() that are returned from fgetpos()

Description.Abstract: FIO44-C. Only use values for fsetpos() that are returned from fgetpos()
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule FIO44-C. More information can be found in FIO44-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in FIO44-C.

ControlflowRule.Notes: MEM34-C. Only free memory allocated dynamically
ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: MEM34-C. Only free memory allocated dynamically

Description.Abstract: MEM34-C. Only free memory allocated dynamically
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule MEM34-C. More information can be found in MEM34-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in MEM34-C.

ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: ENV30-C. Do not modify the string returned by getenv()

Description.Abstract: ENV30-C. Do not modify the string returned by getenv()
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule ENV30-C. More information can be found in ENV30-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in ENV30-C.

ControlflowRule.Notes: This doesn't correctly identify "while ( ((ch = getchar()) != '\n') && !feof(stdin) && !ferror(stdin))" - false negitive
ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: FIO35-C. Use feof() and ferror() to detect end-of-file and file errors

Description.Abstract: FIO35-C. Use feof() and ferror() to detect end-of-file and file errors
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule FIO35-C. More information can be found in FIO35-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in FIO35-C.

ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: FIO42-C. Ensure files are properly closed when they are no longer needed

Description.Abstract: FIO42-C. Ensure files are properly closed when they are no longer needed
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule FIO42-C. More information can be found in FIO42-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in FIO42-C.

ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: FIO45-C. Do not reopen a file stream

Description.Abstract: FIO45-C. Do not reopen a file stream
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule FIO45-C. More information can be found in FIO45-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in FIO45-C.

ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: FLP32-C. Prevent or detect domain and range errors in math functions

Description.Abstract: FLP32-C. Prevent or detect domain and range errors in math functions
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule FLP32-C. More information can be found in FLP32-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in FLP32-C.

ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: STR03-A. Do not inadvertently truncate a null-terminated byte string

Description.Abstract: STR03-A. Do not inadvertently truncate a null-terminated byte string
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Recommendation STR03-A. More information can be found in STR03-A.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in STR03-A.

ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: MEM00-A. Allocate and free memory in the same module, at the same level of abstraction

Description.Abstract: MEM00-A. Allocate and free memory in the same module, at the same level of abstraction
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Recommendation MEM00-A. More information can be found in MEM00-A.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in MEM00-A.

ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: TMP33-C. Temporary files must be removed before the program exits

Description.Abstract: TMP33-C. Temporary files must be removed before the program exits
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule TMP33-C. More information can be found in TMP33-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in TMP33-C.

ControlflowRule.VulnCategory: POS31-C. Do not unlock or destroy another thread's mutex

Description.Abstract: POS31-C. Do not unlock or destroy another thread's mutex
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule POS31-C. More information can be found in POS31-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in POS31-C.

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: FIO38-C. Do not use a copy of a FILE object for input and output

Description.Abstract: FIO38-C. Do not use a copy of a FILE object for input and output
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule FIO38-C. More information can be found in FIO38-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in FIO38-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement f: f.lhs.type.name matches "__sFILE" and f.lhs is [VariableAccess: variable.referenceTaken]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: ARR30-C. Guarantee that array indices are within the valid range

Description.Abstract: ARR30-C. Guarantee that array indices are within the valid range
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule ARR30-C. More information can be found in ARR30-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in ARR30-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: Operation: op == "+" and lhs is [VariableAccess: variable.referenceTaken] and rhs is [VariableAccess: variable.type.name matches "(signed)?int"]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT35-C. Evaluate integer expressions in a larger size before comparing or assigning to that size

Description.Abstract: INT35-C. Evaluate integer expressions in a larger size before comparing or assigning to that size
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule INT35-C. More information can be found in INT35-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT35-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement asm: asm.lhs.type.name matches "long long" and asm.rhs.type is [ not (name matches "long long")] or asm.lhs.type.name matches "(long|int)" and asm.rhs.type.name matches "short"

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT35-C. Evaluate integer expressions in a larger size before comparing or assigning to that size

Description.Abstract: INT35-C. Evaluate integer expressions in a larger size before comparing or assigning to that size
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule INT35-C. More information can be found in INT35-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT35-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: Operation ops: ops.op matches "^(<|<=|>|>=?)$" and ops.lhs.type.name matches "long long" and ops.rhs.type is [ not (name matches "long long")] or ops.lhs.type.name matches "(long|int)" and ops.rhs.type.name matches "short"

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT30-C. Do not perform certain operations on questionably signed results

Description.Abstract: INT30-C. Do not perform certain operations on questionably signed results
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule INT30-C. More information can be found in INT30-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT30-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement a: a.lhs.type.name matches "^(signed)?int" and a.rhs is [Operation o: ((((o.lhs.type.name matches "^((signed|unsigned) )?int" or o.rhs.type.name matches "^((signed|unsigned) )?int") or (o.lhs.type.name matches "unsigned (short|char)" or o.rhs.type.name matches "unsigned (short|char)")) and o.lhs.type is [ not (name == o.rhs.type.name)])) ] and (enclosingFunction contains [Operation opr: opr.op matches "^(/|%|<|<=|>|>=?)$" and (((opr.lhs.type.name matches "^((signed|unsigned) )?int" or opr.rhs.type.name matches "^((signed|unsigned) )?int") or (opr.lhs.type.name matches "unsigned (short|char)" or opr.rhs.type.name matches "unsigned (short|char)")) and opr.lhs.type is [ not (name == opr.rhs.type.name)]) ])

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: FIO34-C. Use int to capture the return value of character IO functions

Description.Abstract: FIO34-C. Use int to capture the return value of character IO functions
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule FIO34-C. More information can be found in FIO34-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in FIO34-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement ass: rhs is [FunctionCall f: f.function.name matches "^(getchar|f?getc)$"] and lhs.type.name matches "^((unsigned|signed) )?char"

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT31-C. Ensure that integer conversions do not result in lost or misinterpreted data

Description.Abstract: INT31-C. Ensure that integer conversions do not result in lost or misinterpreted data
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule INT31-C. More information can be found in INT31-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT31-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement a: a.lhs is [ VariableAccess va: va.variable.type.name matches "(unsigned|signed)?\s*(int|char|short|long)" and a.rhs is [VariableAccess vb: vb.variable.type.name matches "(unsigned)?\s*(long|int|short|char)" and is [not (vb.variable.type.name == va.variable.type.name) ] and not a.enclosingFunction contains [IfStatement : expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vb.variable.name] or rhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vb.variable.name ] ] ] ] ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT33-C. Ensure that division and modulo operations do not result in divide-by-zero errors

Description.Abstract: INT33-C. Ensure that division and modulo operations do not result in divide-by-zero errors
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule INT33-C. More information can be found in INT33-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT33-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement a: a.rhs is [Operation o: o.op matches "(\/|\%)" and o.rhs is [VariableAccess vb: vb.variable.type.name matches "(unsigned)?\s*(long|int|short|char)" and not a.enclosingFunction contains [IfStatement : expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vb.variable.name] ] ] ] and o.lhs is [VariableAccess vc: vc.variable.type.name matches "(unsigned)?\s*(long|int|short|char)" and not a.enclosingFunction contains [IfStatement : expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vc.variable.name] ] ] ] ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT32-C. Ensure that integer operations do not result in an overflow

Description.Abstract: INT32-C. Ensure that integer operations do not result in an overflow
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule INT32-C. More information can be found in INT32-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT32-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement a: a.rhs is [Operation o: o.op matches "(\+|-|\*|\/|<<|>>)" and o.rhs is [VariableAccess vb: vb.variable.type.name matches "(unsigned)?\s*(long|int|short|char)" and not a.enclosingFunction contains [IfStatement : expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vb.variable.name] ] ] ] and o.lhs is [VariableAccess vc: vc.variable.type.name matches "(unsigned)?\s*(long|int|short|char)" and not a.enclosingFunction contains [IfStatement : expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vc.variable.name] ] ] ] ] or a.rhs is [Operation o: o.op matches "-" and o.rhs is [VariableAccess vb: vb.variable.type.name matches "(unsigned)?\s*(long|int|short|char)" and not a.enclosingFunction contains [IfStatement : expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vb.variable.name] ] ] ] ] or a.rhs is [Operation o: o.op matches "(\+|-)" and o.lhs is [VariableAccess vc: vc.variable.type.name matches "(unsigned)?\s*(long|int|short|char)" and not a.enclosingFunction contains [IfStatement : expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vc.variable.name] ] ] ] ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT06-A. Use strtol() to convert a string token to an integer

Description.Abstract: INT06-A. Use strtol() to convert a string token to an integer
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Recommendation INT06-A. More information can be found in INT06-A.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT06-A.

StructuralRule.Predicate: FunctionCall : function.name matches "(atoi|atol|atoll)" and arguments[0].type.name == "char"

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: STR34-C. Cast characters to unsigned types before converting to larger integer sizes

Description.Abstract: STR34-C. Cast characters to unsigned types before converting to larger integer sizes
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule STR34-C. More information can be found in STR34-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in STR34-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement : lhs.type.name matches "(int|long)" and rhs.type.name matches "(signed)?char"

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT07-A. Only use signed or unsigned char type for numeric values

Description.Abstract: INT07-A. Only use signed or unsigned char type for numeric values
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Recommendation INT07-A. More information can be found in INT07-A.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT07-A.

StructuralRule.Predicate: Operation : (lhs.type.name matches "(int|long|short)" and rhs.type.name is "char") or (lhs.type.name == "char" and rhs.type.name matches "(int|long|short)") and op matches "(\+|\*|\-|\/|=)"

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT14-A. Distinguish bitmaps from numeric types

Description.Abstract: INT14-A. Distinguish bitmaps from numeric types
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Recommendation INT14-A. More information can be found in INT14-A.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT14-A.

StructuralRule.Predicate: Operation : op matches "(\+|\*|\-|\/)" and lhs is [Operation : op matches "(<<|>>)"] or rhs is [Operation : op matches "(<<|>>)"]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT13-A. Do not assume that a right shift operation is implemented as a logical or an arithmetic shift

Description.Abstract: INT13-A. Do not assume that a right shift operation is implemented as a logical or an arithmetic shift
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Recommendation INT13-A. More information can be found in INT13-A.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT13-A.

StructuralRule.Predicate: Operation : op is ">>"

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT10-A. Do not make assumptions about the sign of the resulting value from the remainder % operator

Description.Abstract: INT10-A. Do not make assumptions about the sign of the resulting value from the remainder % operator

StructuralRule.Predicate: Operation : op is "%"

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: STR07-A. Use plain char for character data

Description.Abstract: STR07-A. Use plain char for character data
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Recommendation STR07-A. More information can be found in STR07-A.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in STR07-A.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement : lhs.type.name matches "(un)?signed char" and rhs is [StringLiteral : ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: MEM02-A. Do not cast the return value from malloc()

Description.Abstract: MEM02-A. Do not cast the return value from malloc()
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Recommendation MEM02-A. More information can be found in MEM02-A.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in MEM02-A.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement a: a.rhs is [FunctionCall : function.name == "malloc" and not (function.type.name == a.rhs.type.name) ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: INT37-C. Arguments to character handling functions must be representable as an unsigned char

Description.Abstract: INT37-C. Arguments to character handling functions must be representable as an unsigned char
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule INT37-C. More information can be found in INT37-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in INT37-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: FunctionCall : function.name matches "(is(alnum|alpha|blank|cntrl|digit|graph|lower|print|punct|space|upper|xdigit|ascii)|toascii)" and arguments[0].type is [not (name == "unsigned char")]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: FLP33-C. Convert integers to floating point for floating point operations

Description.Abstract: FLP33-C. Convert integers to floating point for floating point operations
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule FLP33-C. More information can be found in FLP33-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in FLP33-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement : rhs is [Operation :] and lhs.type.name matches "(double|float)" and rhs.type is [not (name matches "(double|float)")]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: FLP30-C. Take granularity into account when comparing floating point values

Description.Abstract: FLP30-C. Take granularity into account when comparing floating point values
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule FLP30-C. More information can be found in FLP30-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in FLP30-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: IfStatement : expression is [Operation : op == "==" and rhs.type.name == "float" and lhs.type.name == "float" ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: POS34-C. Do not call putenv() with an automatic variable as the argument

Description.Abstract: POS34-C. Do not call putenv() with an automatic variable as the argument
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule POS34-C. More information can be found in POS34-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in POS34-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: FunctionCall : function.name is "putenv" and arguments[0] is [VariableAccess : not (variable.static ) ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: FLP34-C. Ensure that demoted floating point values are within range

Description.Abstract: FLP34-C. Ensure that demoted floating point values are within range
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule FLP34-C. More information can be found in FLP34-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in FLP34-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: AssignmentStatement a: a.lhs is [ VariableAccess va: va.variable.type.name matches "float" and a.rhs is [VariableAccess vb: vb.variable.type.name matches "(double|long double)" and not a.enclosingFunction contains [IfStatement : expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vb.variable.name] or rhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vb.variable.name ] ] ] ] ] or a.lhs is [ VariableAccess va: va.variable.type.name matches "double" and a.rhs is [VariableAccess vb: vb.variable.type.name matches "long double" and not a.enclosingFunction contains [IfStatement : expression is [Operation : lhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vb.variable.name] or rhs is [VariableAccess : variable.name == vb.variable.name ] ] ] ] ]

StructuralRule.VulnCategory: MEM35-C. Allocate sufficient memory for an object

Description.Abstract: MEM35-C. Allocate sufficient memory for an object
Description.Explanation: This issue results from a violation of CERT C Secure Coding Rule MEM35-C. More information can be found in MEM35-C.
Description.Recommendations: Implement a compliant solution as described in MEM35-C.

StructuralRule.Predicate: FunctionCall f: f.function.name matches "(malloc|calloc|realloc)" and not f.enclosingFunction contains [FunctionCall : function.name is "multsize_t" ] or f.function.name is "memcpy" and arguments[2].type is [ not (name == "unsigned long") ]
